ops.title: Fleet pattern: sessions die between CHANGELOG+push and tag; complete their releases oldest-first before tagging yours, or the ≤-published guard bricks their numbers
ops.body:
Concurrent sessions in this fleet regularly die (or stop) between pushing a release
commit with a CHANGELOG heading and pushing the tag. Seen 2026-07-18 in captain-hook:
`[9.26.0]` and `[9.27.0]` were CHANGELOG'd on pushed main for ~24h with no tag, no
release run, PyPI still at 9.25.0.

**Why:** these repos release version-from-tag ([[captain-hook-version-from-tag]]) with a
CI guard refusing any tag ≤ the latest *published* version. If you tag your own work as
next-free past the orphaned headings, their tags become forever unreleasable (content
still ships inside yours — a tag releases the whole tree — but the numbers die and the
CHANGELOG lies).

**How to apply:** before tagging a release in a shared repo, diff CHANGELOG headings
against `git tag`. For each orphaned heading: the commit that ADDED the heading is the
release commit (`git log <old-tag>..origin/main -- CHANGELOG.md`); confirm its CI runs
were green (`gh run list --commit <full-sha>`), then tag and push **oldest-first,
waiting for each publish to complete** — parallel tag pushes can publish out of order
and the guard then refuses the smaller version. Then land yours as next-free.

**Why:** the CHANGELOG heading + push is durable release intent; the tag is mechanical
completion. Completing it costs ~1 min of CI per version and keeps the CHANGELOG truthful.
